perturbation.original_text: 2.5 Distributor's appointment is for a term of one year from the Effective Date, renewable automatically unless terminated under Section 18
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 Distributor's appointment is for a term of one year from the Effective Date, renewable only upon Tripath's written approval, notwithstanding Section 18.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies automatic renewal unless terminated per Section 18. The modification states that renewal is contingent solely on Tripath's written approval and disregards termination clauses within Section 18, creating an in-text contradiction. This makes it ambiguous if the termination clauses still apply. If Tripath does not approve the writing, the clause becomes uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.1 The terms of this Agreement shall be one year from the effective date and will be automatically renewed on each anniversary of the effective date, for a renewal term of one year unless either party provides written notification of its intention not to renew this Agreement at least one month prior to the expiration of the initial or any renewal terms. Tripath will honor all orders acknowledged prior to the effective date of any such termination by nonrenewable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.5, stating that renewal is 'only upon Tripath's written approval, notwithstanding Section 18,' directly contradicts the automatic renewal clause in Section 18.1, which stipulates automatic renewal unless either party provides written notification of its intention not to renew. The change removes the distributor's right to automatic renewal and places it solely at Tripath's discretion, creating a clear and irreconcilable conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 3.5 Distributor has no authorization to make, and will not make, any guarantee or warranty with respect to any Product — all Product warranties are to be communicated to Customers directly by Tripath in writing. Distributor will make no representations as to quality, performance, capabilities, and the like except as are expressly authorized in writing by Tripath (such as in Tripath's standard, published specifications for a Product).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5 Distributor is authorized to make guarantees and warranties concerning Product capabilities, performance, and quality. Distributor is solely responsible for communicating these warranties to customers.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, distributors are disallowed from making any guarantees or warranties. But now, the altered text permits distributors to guarantee quality, performance, and capabilities. This is in direct contradiction. This shift in responsibility creates uncertainty. Previously, Tripath was responsible for all warranties; now, it’s unclear which party handles them. This contradicts the original allocation of warranty responsibility, causing ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.6 For OEM Products, Tripath provides only the warranties set forth in this limited one year limited warranty. For retail Products, Tripath provides to the end user only the warranties set forth in the applicable end user warranty. Any and all other warranties, either express, implied or statutory, including, without limitation, warranties of fitness for a particular purpose, or of merchantability, are hereby expressly excluded and disclaimed by Tripath.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the distributor from making any guarantees or warranties regarding the product. The changed text now authorizes the distributor to make guarantees and warranties concerning the product's capabilities, performance, and quality. This directly contradicts section 10.6, which states that Tripath provides specific warranties and expressly excludes all other warranties. By allowing the distributor to provide additional warranties, the changed text creates a clear conflict regarding which warranties are valid and who is responsible for them, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability of warranty terms.
